lostDestinations.source: Bert Mooney Airport Wikipedia; Northwest Airlines service ended Butte 1983
Bert Mooney Airport Wikipedia; Northwest Airlines terminated Butte service 1983
Bert Mooney Airport Wikipedia; Western Airlines merged into Delta 1987, jet service 1968-circa 1980
Bert Mooney Airport Wikipedia; Western Air Express served Butte from 1928

note: Northwest Airlines terminated Butte service in 1983; Western Airlines initiated jet service 1968, acquired by Delta 1987; today only DEN and SLC served

lostDestinations.source: Yellowstone Airport Wikipedia; Western's 737 service ended after summer 1980
Yellowstone Airport Wikipedia; Western operated summer 1973 737 service to western cities including SF connections
Yellowstone Airport Wikipedia; Western 737 summer 1973 had Las Vegas connections
Yellowstone Airport Wikipedia; Frontier served 1967-1986 with summer seasonal direct Denver flights

note: West Yellowstone is seasonal summer-only airport; SkyWest/Delta Connection SLC service continues today; Western Airlines ended summer 1980; Frontier (1950-1986) served 1967-1986

lostDestinations.source: Montana Free Press, Nov 25 2024: Helena airport director noted desire to 'bring back a flight to Minneapolis'
Helena Regional Airport Wikipedia; Northwest served Helena from 1936, DC-10/727 era through mid-1980s
Helena Regional Airport Wikipedia; Horizon Air served Montana markets including Helena-Portland

detail: Helena lost Minneapolis service (Delta Connection) circa 2015 and now has only 3 nonstop destinations (SLC, DEN, SEA); airport director stated in 2024 they are 'struggling to expand routes' and seeking to restore Minneapolis and add Chicago or Dallas.
source: Montana Free Press, Nov 25 2024: 'Helena airport struggling to expand routes'

lostDestinations.source: Great Falls International Airport Wikipedia; Frontier pulled out of Great Falls December 2014 during Denver hub restructuring
Great Falls International Airport Wikipedia; Northwest DC-10 service EWR-DTW-ORD-BIL-GTF-GEG-SEA operated 1977-1981

detail: Great Falls lost Frontier's Denver nonstop in December 2014 when Frontier restructured its Denver hub; earlier lost Northwest DC-10 Chicago/eastbound mainline service circa 1981; currently served by Alaska, Delta, United, and Allegiant with no Chicago or Dallas nonstop.
source: Great Falls International Airport Wikipedia; searchresults confirmed Frontier exit Dec 2014

lostDestinations.source: Cut Bank Municipal Airport Wikipedia; cutbankairport.org history
Cut Bank Municipal Airport Wikipedia; Western Airlines served Cut Bank from 1941, discontinued late 1960s

detail: Western Airlines began DC-3 commercial service on June 1 1941 and discontinued flights in the late 1960s; no scheduled commercial service has operated since; airport listed on National Register of Historic Places 2007.
source: cutbankairport.org/about; Cut Bank Municipal Airport Wikipedia

lostDestinations.source: Big Sky Airlines Wikipedia; Big Sky served small Montana cities including Dillon circa early 1980s
Big Sky Airlines Wikipedia; Big Sky route network included Butte-Dillon segment

detail: Dillon Airport (KDLN) is currently general aviation only; scheduled service ended by mid-1980s when Big Sky Airlines contracted its route network; 2019 data shows 83% GA and 16% air taxi operations with no scheduled commercial flights.
source: Dillon Airport Wikipedia; Big Sky Airlines Wikipedia
